speaker: HASTINGS
text: Now, noble peers, the cause why we are met

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Is to determine of the coronation.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: In God’s name, speak. When is the royal day?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Is all things ready for the royal time?

speaker: STANLEY
text: It is, and wants but nomination.

speaker: ELY
text: Tomorrow, then, I judge a happy day.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Who knows the Lord Protector’s mind herein?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Who is most inward with the noble duke?

speaker: ELY
text: Your Grace, we think, should soonest know his mind.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: We know each other’s faces; for our hearts,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: He knows no more of mine than I of yours,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Or I of his, my lord, than you of mine.—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Lord Hastings, you and he are near in love.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I thank his Grace, I know he loves me well.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: But for his purpose in the coronation,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I have not sounded him, nor he delivered

speaker: HASTINGS
text: His gracious pleasure any way therein.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: But you, my honorable lords, may name the time,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And in the Duke’s behalf I’ll give my voice,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Which I presume he’ll take in gentle part.

speaker: ELY
text: In happy time here comes the Duke himself.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My noble lords and cousins all, good morrow.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I have been long a sleeper; but I trust

speaker: RICHARD
text: My absence doth neglect no great design

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which by my presence might have been concluded.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Had you not come upon your cue, my lord,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: William Lord Hastings had pronounced your part—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I mean your voice for crowning of the King.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Than my Lord Hastings no man might be bolder.

speaker: RICHARD
text: His Lordship knows me well and loves me well.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: My lord of Ely, when I was last in Holborn

speaker: RICHARD
text: I saw good strawberries in your garden there;

speaker: RICHARD
text: I do beseech you, send for some of them.

speaker: ELY
text: Marry and will, my lord, with all my heart.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Cousin of Buckingham, a word with you.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Catesby hath sounded Hastings in our business

speaker: RICHARD
text: And finds the testy gentleman so hot

speaker: RICHARD
text: That he will lose his head ere give consent

speaker: RICHARD
text: His master’s child, as worshipfully he terms it,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Shall lose the royalty of England’s throne.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Withdraw yourself awhile. I’ll go with you.

speaker: STANLEY
text: We have not yet set down this day of triumph.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Tomorrow, in my judgment, is too sudden,

speaker: STANLEY
text: For I myself am not so well provided

speaker: STANLEY
text: As else I would be, were the day prolonged.

speaker: ELY
text: Where is my lord the Duke of Gloucester?

speaker: ELY
text: I have sent for these strawberries.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: His Grace looks cheerfully and smooth this morning.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: There’s some conceit or other likes him well

speaker: HASTINGS
text: When that he bids good morrow with such spirit.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I think there’s never a man in Christendom

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Can lesser hide his love or hate than he,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: For by his face straight shall you know his heart.

speaker: STANLEY
text: What of his heart perceive you in his face

speaker: STANLEY
text: By any livelihood he showed today?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Marry, that with no man here he is offended,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: For were he, he had shown it in his looks.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I pray you all, tell me what they deserve

speaker: RICHARD
text: That do conspire my death with devilish plots

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of damnèd witchcraft, and that have prevailed

speaker: RICHARD
text: Upon my body with their hellish charms?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: The tender love I bear your Grace, my lord,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Makes me most forward in this princely presence

speaker: HASTINGS
text: To doom th’ offenders, whosoe’er they be.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I say, my lord, they have deservèd death.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then be your eyes the witness of their evil.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Look how I am bewitched! Behold mine arm

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is like a blasted sapling withered up;

speaker: RICHARD
text: And this is Edward’s wife, that monstrous witch,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Consorted with that harlot, strumpet Shore,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That by their witchcraft thus have markèd me.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: If they have done this deed, my noble lord—

speaker: RICHARD
text: If? Thou protector of this damnèd strumpet,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Talk’st thou to me of “ifs”? Thou art a traitor.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Off with his head. Now by Saint Paul I swear

speaker: RICHARD
text: I will not dine until I see the same.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Lovell and Ratcliffe, look that it be done.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: The rest that love me, rise and follow me.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Woe, woe for England! Not a whit for me,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: For I, too fond, might have prevented this.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Stanley did dream the boar did raze his helm,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And I did scorn it and disdain to fly.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Three times today my foot-cloth horse did stumble,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And started when he looked upon the Tower,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: As loath to bear me to the slaughterhouse.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: O, now I need the priest that spake to me!

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I now repent I told the pursuivant,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: As too triumphing, how mine enemies

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Today at Pomfret bloodily were butchered,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And I myself secure in grace and favor.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: O Margaret, Margaret, now thy heavy curse

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Is lighted on poor Hastings’ wretched head.

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Come, come, dispatch. The Duke would be at dinner.

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Make a short shrift. He longs to see your head.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: O momentary grace of mortal men,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Which we more hunt for than the grace of God!

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Who builds his hope in air of your good looks

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Lives like a drunken sailor on a mast,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Ready with every nod to tumble down

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Into the fatal bowels of the deep.

speaker: LOVELL
text: Come, come, dispatch. ’Tis bootless to exclaim.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: O bloody Richard! Miserable England,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I prophesy the fearfull’st time to thee

speaker: HASTINGS
text: That ever wretched age hath looked upon.—

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Come, lead me to the block. Bear him my head.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: They smile at me who shortly shall be dead.
